claim.content: Providing an agent with access to a curated set of MCP tools—defined as callable programmatic interfaces that perform application-level or OS-level operations—and making those tools selectable at each action step causally increases the agent’s task success rate (accuracy) and typically reduces Average Completion Steps (ACS) for most evaluated large multimodal models under the stated experimental conditions, when other experimental factors (agent configuration, model version, and step limits) are held constant, so that the deliberate change between conditions is the presence versus absence of MCP tools.
